title: Silky Cozy Vietnamese Chicken Pho
description: A comforting bowl of silky chicken pho infused with fermented fish sauce, a dash of lime, and a whisked eggwhite swirl for a silky texture.
instructions: Bring the water to a simmer in a stockpot.
Add the sliced ginger and onion and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the chicken bouillon powder until fully dissolved.
Add the chicken breast and cook until done (about 5 minutes), then remove the chicken from the pot.
Shred or cut the cooked chicken into bite‑sized pieces and set aside.
Add fermented fish sauce and lime juice to the pot, simmer 2 minutes to meld flavors.
In a small bowl, whisk the egg white until frothy, then slowly pour into the hot broth while whisking continuously to create a silky, glossy texture.
Add the pre‑boiled rice noodles to the pot and heat through for 1 minute.
Plate the noodles, top with shredded chicken, scallions, cilantro and a squeeze of lime.
Serve hot with extra lime wedges and fish sauce at the table.
Enjoy the silky, cozy bowl of Vietnamese chicken pho.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
